title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. scribneri
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. scribneri, also known as Scribner's needlegrass, is a subspecies of the Elymus trachycaulus plant, a type of grass native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its essential oils and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Scribner's needlegrass are thought to include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, or infused in oil to create topical applications for skin and wound care.
